analysis.domain_summary: Customer support tickets: short-to-medium text (emails, chat logs, ticket fields) labeled by issue type (billing, technical, account, shipping, etc.) and urgency/severity. Useful datasets should include clear labels or metadata, diverse channels (email/chat/phone transcripts), multiple organizations/verticals to reduce overfitting, and enough volume to cover common and edge-case issue classes. Expect noise, informal language, truncated contexts, threaded messages, and potential PII that must be removed.
analysis.quality_tier_strategy: Balanced (tier 3): aim to combine 2–3 complementary datasets totaling 50K–500K rows. Prefer one core, well-labeled ticket dataset for issue-type labels plus one dataset with threaded conversations or urgency annotations for edge cases. Apply label mapping to a common taxonomy, class balancing or resampling, and lightweight augmentation (paraphrasing) if gaps remain. Prioritize datasets with clear licenses and documented labeling guidelines; filter and PII-scrub before use.

search_queries.intent: Find core labeled ticket corpora with issue and/or urgency labels to form the primary dataset.
Identify well-liked, curated ticket datasets that may include multi-class labels or structured metadata.
Locate threaded helpdesk/chat logs to capture multi-turn context and urgency cues for edge-case labeling.
Find email corpora with subject/body useful for issue-type classification and extracting urgency signals.
Target vertical-specific tickets (IT) to improve coverage of technical categories and terminology.
Discover recent conversational datasets that capture modern chat-style support interactions.
Find incident/issue reports that emphasize severity/urgency labels to bootstrap urgency classification.
Search for datasets annotated with multiple labels (issue type + urgency) to simplify mapping to the target taxonomy.

ranking_criteria.description: Datasets that include explicit issue-type and/or urgency/severity labels, or rich metadata that maps easily to the target taxonomy, rank highest.
Prefer datasets with enough labeled examples to contribute meaningfully toward the 50K+ target; larger single datasets reduce need for heavy augmentation.
Clear annotation guidelines, inter-annotator agreement, and label definitions reduce downstream noise and relabeling effort.
Mix of emails, chat logs, and ticket fields improves generalization; vertical diversity (retail, SaaS, telco) helps coverage of varied issue types.

ranking_criteria.criterion: Class balance and coverage of edge cases
ranking_criteria.description: Datasets that cover both common and rare issue types and provide urgency labels or proxies are more valuable, even if smaller.
Prefer permissive/legal-clear datasets; high PII risk requires more preprocessing and can reduce usable rows.

recommendation_guidance.mixing_strategy: Use the selected dataset as the primary source and only add a second dataset if GPT identifies a clear coverage gap.
warnings: PII/leak risk: support tickets commonly contain personal data—perform mandatory PII detection and removal.
Label mismatch: differing taxonomies across datasets will require mapping and may introduce noisy labels.
License incompatibility: ensure dataset licenses allow your intended use (commercial vs research).
Class imbalance: urgent/severe classes are often underrepresented—plan for augmentation or targeted collection.
Duplicate/near-duplicate records: merging multiple sources can create duplicates that bias training.
License is CC-BY-NC-4.0 (non-commercial) — verify this meets your intended use (commercial use may be restricted).

A deterministic text-classification normalization requires an explicit label column (or clear metadata) to map to training targets; synthesizing labels would be nondeterministic. The identifier column appears to be an internal ticket ID (possible PII/identifier) and should not be used as a label. Therefore a usable deterministic normalization for supervised SFT cannot be produced.

While 'issue_type' and 'priority' are useful labels for text-classification, there is no message body, description, or transcript column to use as the input text. Additionally, several columns are likely identifiers/PII (ticket_id, customer_id, assigned_to) and should be avoided unless essential. recommended_datasets.why: Best single, ready-to-use dataset balancing task fit, deterministic normalization, label coverage (type/queue/priority), and clear license metadata. It contains ~61.8k labeled tickets (subject/body/priority/type/queue/answer), is classification-ready via 'body'+'priority', supports multi-task uses (classification, QA, summarization), and has deterministic prompt/completion normalization—making it a compact, high-quality core dataset for post-training and fine-tuning.
